config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, a healthy society is built by balancing both, not by sacrificing personal freedom whenever it is inconvenient. From my perspective, the best common good comes from respecting people first, then asking them to contribute to society responsibly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. Coming from China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, especially after seeing how disorder can hurt ordinary lives, but I also think that without real individual rights, "society" can become an excuse for power to silence people. The common good is important, yet it is stronger when it is built on protecting basic rights first, not when those rights are treated as optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because once that rule is accepted, it becomes too easy for authorities or majorities to justify abuse in the name of the common good. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of social harmony, mutual duty, and keeping order, but I have also seen how "for the good of society" can be used to pressure people into silence instead of solving problems fairly. A good society is not one that crushes personal rights for convenience, but one that protects those rights while still asking people to act responsibly toward others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, social stability and collective responsibility are very important, but I have also seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify silence, pressure, or abuse if individual rights are not protected first. A society is stronger when it protects basic rights and then asks people to contribute responsibly, not when it treats personal rights as something that can be ignored whenever authorities say it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because in practice that idea often gives authorities and majorities too much power to define what “society” even means. From an Indonesian perspective, I do value gotong royong, harmony, and responsibility to others, but I do not think those values justify sacrificing basic rights or forcing silence in the name of order. A healthy society is one that protects individual dignity first and then builds cooperation on top of that, not one that treats personal rights as disposable whenever collective goals are invoked.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter greatly, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, because social disorder can hurt ordinary people very quickly; still, I do not accept the idea that “society” can be used as a blank check to override basic rights. In practice, once individual rights are treated as secondary, it becomes too easy for authorities or majorities to silence criticism and call it the common good. A strong society is one that protects individual dignity first and then asks people to contribute responsibly, not one that sacrifices rights whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
